patch.value:
## Problem
Maintainer passes read `pm validate --json` on every session purely for the drift COUNTS (missing resolution, close_reason, linked-file drift). Today that read costs the full row arrays: the resolution check emits all 269 missing_resolution_rows and the files check emits up to file_list_summary_limit (40) entries per list even when the caller only needs missing_linked_paths_count / orphaned_paths_count. For agents this is hundreds of wasted tokens per verification read, on one of the most-run diagnostic commands.

## Proposal
A counts-only projection for validate output: keep check names, statuses, and every *_count / *_total scalar; drop per-item row arrays and classification lists. Spelling candidates (pick one at implementation): `pm validate --counts`, `--details none`, or reuse the lean-output mode of GH-435 (pm-cfed) if that lands first with array-suppression semantics.

## Non-overlap
- pm-cfed (GH-435) lean JSON drops null/EMPTY fields — these row arrays are non-empty, so it does not cover this.
- pm-vxxm (GH-442) is contracts --summary, a different command.
- pm-edge (closed) trimmed validate required_fields to active profiles; row arrays remained.
- The row worklists themselves are a feature (exact missing-metadata ID worklists, see ADR-era usage on pm-bl8x) — this adds a cheaper projection, it removes nothing.

## Evidence
Session 2026-07-07: drift verification needed exactly four scalars (269/18/147/125) and paid for ~300 row entries across two reads; the *_count fields already exist, so the projection is pure output filtering.
pm validate needs a counts-only projection so agents can read drift numbers without row-array payloads
Contracts, MCP pm_validate params, completions, and docs updated per the new-flag wiring checklist

pm validate --json supports a counts-only mode (flag or details=summary) keeping every *_count/*_total field and dropping per-item row arrays (missing_resolution_rows, missing_linked_path_rows, orphaned_path_rows, duplicate lists); human-readable output gains the same projection; default behavior unchanged (full row worklists remain default); contracts, MCP pm_validate params, completions, and docs updated per the new-flag wiring checklist

author: codex-root

patch.value: Add a counts-only validate projection that preserves diagnostic totals while omitting large per-item row arrays across CLI, MCP, contracts, and docs.

message: Backfill concise active-item definition from existing title, acceptance criteria, and live audit evidence

author: maintainer-agent
message: Graph pass #92: counts-only validate projection and envelope-verbosity reduction are the same output-slimming class on read surfaces

author: claude-code-agent
message: Start broad SDK-first bounded and streamable projection implementation

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implementation start 2026-07-23: actively deliver the shared bounded/streamable read projection surface across pm-gygna8, pm-a9mc, pm-ishm, and pm-646c. Scope includes public SDK contracts, thin CLI/MCP wiring, deterministic bounds, token-efficient projections, pipeline behavior, docs, contracts, focused regressions, exact coverage, and packed/manual consumer proof. All-status duplicate review reused these canonical items and their closed prerequisites; no parallel implementation item was created.

author: claude-code-agent
message: Link counts projection to bounded dependency projection

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implementation evidence: validate --counts and projectValidateCounts preserve the gate envelope, warnings, scalar totals, and nested count maps while recursively omitting diagnostic/fix row arrays; focused tests pass.

patch.value: Expose counts-aware public SDK and PmClient overloads without unsafe casts

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Acceptance evidence: packed tarball installed into /tmp/tmp.2S2yySAQe2/consumer; validate --counts retained scalar/count-map evidence, removed diagnostic arrays recursively, and public SDK projectValidateCounts export passed. Public overloads now distinguish ValidateResult from ValidateCountsResult.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Final verification evidence: full sandboxed repository coverage passed 349 files / 6,189 tests at exact Statements 42,435/42,435, Branches 30,059/30,059, Functions 8,645/8,645, Lines 41,245/41,245. Typecheck, ESLint, zero-duplication, static quality, 100% docstrings, package boundary, token budgets, docs/skills, contracts, context evaluation, linked tests, and packed consumer acceptance also passed.

patch.value: Implemented counts-only validation projection across CLI/MCP/public SDK with accurate typed result overloads, packed acceptance, docs, and exact coverage.
Validation offers a token-efficient counts projection across CLI, MCP, and public SDK without weakening full-result types.
Recursive row-array omission preserves scalar totals/count maps, contracts expose --counts, and typed ValidateCountsResult overloads pass packed SDK acceptance.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review-round evidence: exported ValidateCountsResult from the primary SDK barrel so the counts:true overload return type is directly importable. Typecheck, SDK index tests, and exact full coverage pass.
